title: Epitaph
content:
Heap not on this mound
                 Roses that she loved so well;
             Why bewilder her with roses,
                 That she cannot see or smell?
             She is happy where she lies
                 With the dust upon her eyes.
meaning: About: Edna St. Vincent Millay was an American lyrical poet and playwright and the first woman to receive the Pulitzer Prize for Poetry. She was also known for her unconventional, bohemian lifestyle and her many love affairs. She used the pseudonym Nancy Boyd for her prose work.
